summary:
Porosity resulting from concurrent grain and bubble nucleation when a metal starts to freeze inside its mold defines what we call micro porosity; it occurs right after casting initiation with significant contributions from trapped gaseous impurities and crystalline formations. \boxed{\text{Micro porosity}} Agent1’s reasoning can be summarized into two sentences focusing on the critical aspects related to the specific type of porosity mentioned:

Porosity caused by simultaneous nucleation of solid grains and gas bubbles just as a metal begins to freeze in a mold is known as micro porosity. It results from immediate interactions between cooling metals and their surroundings leading to small cavity formations early in the solidification process. 

Thus,
\boxed{\text{micro porosity describes such an occurrence where pores form rapidly through combined effects of crystal growth and gas entrapment initially while molten metal cools.}}
